verses.latin_vulgate: Canticum graduum.   Ad Dominum cum tribularer clamavi,  et exaudivit me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁ֗יר הַֽמַּ֫עֲל֥וֹת אֶל־יְ֭הֹוָה בַּצָּרָ֣תָה לִּ֑י קָ֝רָ֗אתִי וַֽיַּעֲנֵֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: In my distress I cried unto the Lord, and he heard me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine, libera animam meam a labiis iniquis  et a lingua dolosa.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְֽהֹוָ֗ה הַצִּ֣ילָה נַ֭פְשִׁי מִשְּׂפַת־שֶׁ֑קֶר מִלָּשׁ֥וֹן רְמִיָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: Deliver my soul, O Lord, from lying lips, and from a deceitful tongue.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid detur tibi, aut quid apponatur tibi  ad linguam dolosam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־יִּתֵּ֣ן לְ֭ךָ וּמַה־יֹּסִ֥יף לָ֗ךְ לָשׁ֥וֹן רְמִיָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: What shall be given unto thee? or what shall be done unto thee, thou false tongue?

verses.latin_vulgate: Sagittæ potentis acutæ,  cum carbonibus desolatoriis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חִצֵּ֣י גִבּ֣וֹר שְׁנוּנִ֑ים עִ֝֗ם גַּחֲלֵ֥י רְתָמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Sharp arrows of the mighty, with coals of juniper.

verses.latin_vulgate: Heu mihi, quia incolatus meus prolongatus est !  habitavi cum habitantibus Cedar ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֽוֹיָה־לִ֭י כִּי־גַ֣רְתִּי מֶ֑שֶׁךְ שָׁ֝כַ֗נְתִּי עִֽם־אׇהֳלֵ֥י קֵדָֽר׃
verses.king_james: Woe is me, that I sojourn in Mesech, that I dwell in the tents of Kedar!

verses.hebrew_masoretic: רַ֭בַּת שָֽׁכְנָה־לָּ֣הּ נַפְשִׁ֑י עִ֝֗ם שׂוֹנֵ֥א שָׁלֽוֹם׃
verses.king_james: My soul hath long dwelt with him that hateth peace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum his qui oderunt pacem eram pacificus ;  cum loquebar illis, impugnabant me gratis.
verses.king_james: I am for peace: but when I speak, they are for war.
